front: Photograph: Depicts a nude woman's back with two f-holes painted on her skin to evoke a musical instrument
Photograph: The model is Kiki de Montparnasse, the 'Queen of Montparnasse' (real name Alice Ernestine Prin)
Photograph: Kiki de Montparnasse's white face resting horizontally next to an upright African mask
Artist: Named his camera-less photographs (photograms) after himself
Artist: Developed the solarization technique together with this lover, whose torso appears in one of his photograms
Photograph: Depicts Meret Oppenheim nude with ink on her palm and forearm at a printing press
back: Man Ray (Meret Oppenheim at the etching press)

front: Artist: Photographed Marcel Duchamp cross-dressed as female alter-ego Rrose Selavy
Artist: Portrait of Rrose Selavy appeared on the label of the Belle Haleine perfume bottle
Installation: A sewing machine wrapped in an army blanket and tied with yellow string; title references Lautréamont
back: The Enigma of Isidore Ducasse (Man Ray)

front: Installation: Metronome with a photograph of an eye on the pendulum; the original was destroyed by student protesters in 1957
Installation: An iron with a row of tacks glued to the bottom; presented at the Cabaret Voltaire
Artist: Born Emmanuel Radnitzky; Dadaist and Surrealist photographer based in Paris
Artist: Berenice Abbott worked as this artist's assistant; both championed Eugène Atget's street photography
Artist: Passionate chess player who designed the first modern chess set of purely abstract geometrical forms
Artist: Self-portrait Autoportrait consists of a handprint on a white pentagon above a doorbell
